title: Adapt ffmpeg audio recorder for session-triggered start/stop
description: Port the meeting-recorder ffmpeg script into a session-aware recorder. Must: auto-detect USB mic device name via 'ffmpeg -list_devices true -f dshow -i dummy', start recording when session starts (S3 polling), stop on session end or 'stop audio' command, output WAV 44100Hz stereo to session folder, handle graceful SIGINT for clean stop. No interactive prompts. Config via environment variables (AUDIO_DEVICE, SESSION_POLL_URL).
tests: Run with no USB mic -> error message with list of available devices
Run with valid device -> WAV file created, 44100Hz stereo verified
Send stop signal -> recording stops cleanly, file is valid (not corrupted)
10-minute recording -> file size reasonable (~100MB WAV), no gaps
Auto-detect picks correct USB mic when multiple audio devices exist
